incorrects.description: Student selected 25: confused the total value with the number of symbols needed

params.description: Juice category highlights on data table.

steps.text: The key says each symbol equals 5. How many 5s make 25? Count by 5s.

incorrects.description: Student selected 4: stopped one group short of the total

params.description: Juice category highlights on data table.

steps.text: You're close. Count by 5s all the way to 25. Does 4 groups of 5 reach 25?

incorrects.description: Student selected 30: chose the next category's value instead of calculating symbols for 25

params.description: Juice category highlights on data table.

steps.text: We're working with Juice, which has 25 votes. Each symbol stands for 5. How many 5s are in 25?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System demonstrates skip-counting by 5s: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25. Five groups highlight sequentially.

steps.text: Let me show you. When the scale is 5, count by 5s to reach 25. That's 5, 10, 15, 20, 25. Five groups of 5 equals 25. So you need 5 symbols for 25 votes. The answer is 5 because counting by 5s five times gives you the total.
